title: Location-based and function-based location filtering
abstract: In some implementations, a device may obtain, via a visual element of a user interface, a request to filter a location element of the user interface by a function, wherein the visual element indicates the function, and wherein the device is associated with a device location. The device may search, based on obtaining the request, first information associated with a set of locations to identify one or more locations that are: one or more physical locations at which the function is performable and that are within a threshold distance from the device location, or one or more digital locations at which the function is performable. The device may provide, for display, the user interface including the location element depicting second information associated with the one or more locations.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system for location-based and function-based location filtering, the system comprising: one or more memories; and one or more processors, communicatively coupled to the one or more memories, configured to: provide, for display, a user interface that includes a location element that indicates physical locations; obtain, via a filter chip of the user interface, a request to filter the location element by a function, wherein the filter chip indicates the function; filter, using a device location associated with the system and the function, one or more locations from a set of locations, wherein filtering using the function includes identifying that the function is performable at the one or more locations; and provide, for display and based on obtaining the request to filter the location element by the function, the location element indicating the one or more locations. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors, to provide the user interface, are configured to: provide, for display, multiple filter chips associated with respective functions, wherein the multiple filter chips are associated with filtering the physical locations indicated by the location element, and wherein the multiple filter chips include the filter chip. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more locations include one or more digital locations. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the function includes a service offered by a location. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more locations include multiple locations associated with different location types. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the user interface is associated with a first entity, and wherein the different location types include at least one of: first one or more location types associated with the first entity, a cash machine associated with the first entity, or 

 second one or more location types associated with a second entity, wherein the second one or more location types provide services for the first entity. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors, to filter the one or more locations from the set of locations, are configured to: identify that a first location, from the set of locations, is located in a second location and that the function is performable at the first location; and include the first location in the one or more locations based on an availability of the second location. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors, to filter the one or more locations from the set of locations, are configured to: identify that a location, from the set of locations, is associated with a location type that supports the function and that the function is performable at the location; obtain information indicating that the function is currently unavailable at the location; and refrain, based on the function being currently unavailable, from including the location in the one or more locations. 9. A method of location-based and function-based location searching, comprising: obtaining, by a device and via a visual element of a user interface, a request to filter a location element of the user interface by a function, wherein the visual element indicates the function, and wherein the device is associated with a device location; searching, by the device and based on obtaining the request, first information associated with a set of locations to identify one or more locations that are: one or more physical locations at which the function is performable and that are within a threshold distance from the device location, or one or more digital locations at which the function is performable; and providing, by the device and for display, the user interface including the location element depicting second information associated with the one or more locations. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: providing, for display via the device, the user interface including multiple visual elements associated with respective functions, wherein the multiple visual elements include the visual element, and wherein obtaining the request comprises: 

 obtaining an indication of a user input selecting the visual element from the multiple visual elements. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the visual element is a fdter chip associated with filtering locations by the function. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the user interface is associated with an application executing on the device, and wherein the one or more digital locations include at least one of: a page of the application, or a webpage associated with an entity that is associated with the application. 13. The method of claim 9, further comprising: obtaining, via the user interface, an indication of a user input selecting a digital location from the one or more digital locations; and navigating, based on obtaining the indication of the user input, to the digital location. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein searching the first information associated with the set of locations comprises: filtering, to obtain a subset of locations, the set of locations by one or more location types that are associated with supporting the function; and determining whether the function is currently performable at each location included in the subset of locations, wherein the one or more locations include locations, from the subset of locations, at which the function is currently performable. 15. The method of claim 9, further comprising: obtaining operational status information associated with a location included in the one or more locations, wherein the operational status information indicates whether a device associated with the location is currently capable of performing the function; and including the location in the one or more locations based on the operational status information indicating that the device is currently capable of performing the function. 16. The method of claim 9, wherein the one or more locations include a first location associated with a physical location and a second location associated with the physical location, wherein the first location is associated with a first location type and the second location is associated with a second location type, and 

 wherein providing the user interface comprises: providing, for display, the location element, wherein the location element includes a map element, wherein the map element includes an indicator associated with the physical location, and wherein the indicator is associated with the first location based on the first location type having a higher priority level than a priority level of the second location type for the function. 17. A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing a set of instructions, the set of instructions comprising: one or more instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a device, cause the device to: provide, for display, a user interface that includes a location element that depicts location indicators; obtain, via a filter chip of the user interface, a request to filter the location element by a function, filter, using a device location associated with the device and the function, one or more locations from a set of locations, wherein the function is performable at the one or more locations; and provide, for display and based on obtaining the request to filter the location element by the function, the location element indicating the one or more locations. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 17, wherein the one or more instructions, that cause the device to provide the user interface, cause the device to: provide, for display, multiple filter chips associated with respective functions, wherein the multiple filter chips are associated with filtering the location indicators, and wherein the multiple filter chips include the filter chip. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 17, wherein the one or more instructions, that cause the device to filter the one or more locations from the set of locations, cause the device to: identify that a first location, from the set of locations, is located in a second location and that the function is performable at the first location; and include the first location in the one or more locations based on an availability of the second location. 20. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 17, wherein the one or more instructions, that cause the device to filter the one or more locations from the set of locations, cause the device to: identify that a location, from the set of locations, is associated with a location type that supports the function and that the function is performable at the location; obtain information indicating that the function is offline at the location; and refrain, based on the function being currently unavailable, from including the location in the one or more locations.
